reasoning: The question asks whether South Korea's fertility rate will increase in 2025 compared to 2023 (0.72). Recent official data from Statistics Korea confirms the 2024 fertility rate rose to 0.75, breaking a 9-year decline. Importantly, marriages surged by 14.9% in 2024 (the largest spike since 1970) due to post-pandemic catch-up, which serves as a very strong leading indicator for births in 2025. Data for the first quarter of 2025 already shows a robust fertility rate of 0.82 (up 7.3% year-on-year from Q1 2024). The Presidential Committee on Ageing Society and Population Policy officially forecasts the 2025 fertility rate to reach 0.79. Given that 2024 strongly beat the 0.72 threshold and the 2024 marriage boom guarantees elevated births in 2025, there is an extraordinarily high probability that 2025 will be higher than 0.72. The 0.04 probability of 'No' is reserved strictly for a catastrophic exogenous shock or massive data revision, which are highly unlikely.
